scope.note: The extraction captures authored direct call tokens. Classification and public-entry evidence are reviewed semantic annotations, not inferred solely from syntax.
scope.id_policy: Internal-call IDs use source path, callee token, and the same-callee ordinal in numeric source-line order. Source lines are non-identity locators, so unrelated edits above a call do not change its ID. Public-entry IDs use the stable entry identity.

replacement.rationale: The deletion-first steering decision retires every non-survivor YAML/YML workflow without translating its internal calls.
The promoted Design Delta .orc family is current guidance; historical parent parity supports the family archive but does not prove child parity or cross-source resume.

queues.stage_6_gate: Design Delta archive gate and Task 6

replacement.rationale: Task 5 closed the dedicated .orc source, typed parity, promotion, fresh-smoke, and ordered-review gates; Task 6 owns the retained YAML twin's reference and supported-run deletion gates.
Task 5 closed the dedicated .orc source, typed parity, promotion, fresh-smoke, and ordered-review gates; Task 6 owns the retained YAML twin's reference and supported-run deletion gates.
The protected in-flight recovery owner must record delete-or-port disposition before Stage 6 mutates this path.

run_consumer_scan_contract.matching_scope: exact queue source paths, including nested call-frame ownership
run_consumer_scan_contract.deletion_rule: zero supported matching nonterminal run or call-frame consumer

records.named_substrate_gap: The completed tracked-plan pilot root retains two completed runs with supported tracked-design-phase old-identity consumers; reviewed_internal_identity_retirement requires consumer_count=0 and no remap/upgrader exists.
records.reason: Retain this internal workflow boundary. A read-only generic scanner witness found 26 supported old-identity consumers in the known completed-pilot store, so the accepted strict identity default forbids inline retirement even though the callee is not public.

notes: The eligibility stop is deterministic and monotonic: a five-identity subset already finds supported consumers, so the complete production query cannot restore the required zero-consumer predicate.
Reconsider only through a separately accepted state-retention/upgrader contract or after the retained consumer store is legitimately retired by its own owner and evidence contract.

records.named_substrate_gap: pure_expr_operand_type_mismatch: the current compiler reduces BlockerClass.roadmap_conflict to String in the minimal inline pure expression while finalize-selected-item-from-blocked-implementation requires std/resource::BlockerClass; no accepted lowering preserves the typed contract.
records.reason: Retain this blocked-finalizer call as an effect adapter because the deterministic exact five-call finalizer conversion fails at the real compiler typecheck boundary before producing an executable.

notes: The fail-closed source override serves only the selected bytes, guards writes, and leaves production bytes unchanged. This retention makes no checkpoint-delta or affected-route runtime parity claim.
